Quality Education.reason: The course is a direct example of tertiary education (Target 4.3) and provides students with relevant skills (Target 4.4) for communication and further learning.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course provides 'essential communication skills' which can improve prospects for employment and economic participation, linking to Target 8.5.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: By teaching the local language, the course promotes the social inclusion (Target 10.2) of non-native speakers, helping to reduce barriers and facilitate integration.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Teaching Mandarin safeguards language as cultural heritage (Target 11.4) and enables participation in community life through 'practical daily expressions'.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course facilitates international knowledge sharing and capacity-building (Targets 17.6, 17.9) by teaching Mandarin to a diverse student body, fostering cross-cultural communication.
